header_summary:
Bitcoin Core version 29.4 introduces numerous enhancements and bug fixes, and it is now available for download. This version addresses several technical issues such as excessive disk reads and writes in the chainstate database and improves transaction data lifetime management and network interfaces. Users are encouraged to upgrade and can report issues on the [GitHub Issues page](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues) and join the announcement mailing list at [Bitcoin Core Announcements](https://bitcoincore.org/en/list/announcements/join/).

In the ongoing development discussions on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List, the idea of enhancing transaction aggregation by allowing multiple groups per scheme was debated but ultimately dismissed due to its complexity and lack of a clear use case. The conversation also explored the potential integration of a 'group ID' into transaction marker bytes to differentiate aggregation types, which could impact privacy by indicating participant groups in transactions. This reflects the community's effort to balance technical enhancements with privacy and system integrity concerns.

Separately, the Bitcoin Inquisition's latest release, version 29.4, based on Bitcoin Core's updates, introduces several consensus changes aimed at enhancing transaction functionality and security, including support for BIP 118 and BIP 119. These updates, which improve transaction flexibility and verification processes, are detailed in their respective GitHub pull requests and have been progressively activated on the default signet, with links to further details available on the [official GitHub repository](https://github.com/bitcoin-inquisition/bitcoin/releases/tag/v29.4-inq) and historical context provided in the December 2022 announcement [here](https://lists.linuxfoundation.org/pipermail/bitcoin-dev/2022-December/021275.html).

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core 29.4 released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 29.4 is now available with numerous updates and bug fixes.
- Users should properly shut down older versions before upgrading to ensure compatibility.
- The release showcases significant improvements across system performance and documentation.

recent_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
recent_posts.summary: - Bitcoin transaction schemes focus on flexibility and aggregation groups.  - Technical challenges and privacy concerns limit changes to aggregation methods.  - Ongoing debates continue to refine blockchain processes and privacy impacts.

recent_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
recent_posts.summary:
- Miners might switch to mining a "revisionist" chain, abandoning the accepted blockchain.
- Mining the revisionist chain compromises system integrity and could invalidate transactions.
- Shallow chain reorganizations pose threats, preventive measures are suggested.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Inquisition 29.4
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Inquisition version 29.4, based on Bitcoin Core 29.4, is now available.
- It adds support for BIPs 118, 119, 347, 348, and 349, enhancing transaction functionality.
- Updates include TEMPLATEHASH (BIP 446), activated July 2026, supporting advanced features.

recent_posts.title: RFC: Block-Range Filters (a.k.a. Hierarchical filters)
recent_posts.summary:
- Advanced data structures enhance set membership query efficiency and adaptability.
- Challenges like txid collisions necessitate lower false positive rates in block queries.
- Deploying AMSMMQ structures in peer-to-peer networks improves synchronization and security.

recent_posts.title: [Research] A clockless vardiff strands a slowing miner
recent_posts.summary:
- Proxies dynamically adjust mining difficulty based on observed mining activity.
- Stratum V2 uses `UpdateChannel` for difficulty changes, differing from Stratum V1's protocols.
- Datum technology integrates difficulty directly into the coinbase, enhancing efficiency.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
active_posts.summary:
- The BIP draft introduces a new signature aggregation method for Bitcoin transactions.
- Detailed specifications are in BIP 458 and BIP 459, addressing different aggregation types.
- Contributors can review and discuss the proposal via provided GitHub links.

active_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
active_posts.summary: - Game-theory analyses suggest strategic issues in Bitcoin's post-quantum security and economics.  - Quantum threats might exploit lack of post-quantum proofs and coordination challenges.  - Research and new strategies are essential to protect blockchain against quantum advancements.

active_posts.title: Bitcoin Core 29.4 released
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 29.4 is now available with numerous enhancements and bug fixes.
- Users can report issues on GitHub and join the Bitcoin Core mailing list.
- The software supports old wallet versions and is compatible with recent operating systems.

active_posts.title: P2share: how to turn any network (or testnet!) into a bitcoin miner
active_posts.summary:
- The "p2share" network changes reward distribution by giving bitcoin to one randomly chosen shareholder.
- This model encourages fairness, efficiency, and maintains a decentralized, trustless mining environment.
- It raises questions about incentive compatibility and its impact on Bitcoin's mainnet security.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- A Bitcoin-native Language Model aims to enhance Bitcoin protocol interaction and understanding.
- This specialized LLM requires a robust dataset from diverse Bitcoin-related discussions and resources.
- The model design combines script analysis with a real-time data querying layer for utility in various scenarios.

active_posts.title: Input-triggered transaction expiry
active_posts.summary:
- The input-triggered expiry mechanism manages transaction validity and enhances consensus robustness.
- Simplifies transaction processes and enhances security through pseudo contract-level relative timelocks.
- Decreases storage and verification requirements by employing `TEMPLATEHASH` and vector commitments.

today_in_history_posts.title: Re: Concern about "Inscriptions"
today_in_history_posts.summary: - A recent email highlights a bug linked to "inscriptions" affecting blockchain functionality.  - No effective controls exist in Bitcoin Core to manage inscriptions impacting the mempool.  - There's a call for Bitcoin developers to enable inscription rejection to maintain network integrity.

today_in_history_posts.title: Blinded Paths Doom Scenario
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Blinded paths in the Lightning Network increase privacy for receiving nodes.
- Compliance pressure may enforce routing through large regulated hubs, reducing privacy.
- The author questions if receiver privacy trade-offs are necessary or solvable.

today_in_history_posts.title: Bolt 12 Trusted Contacts
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bolt 12 enhances lightning wallets by adding user-friendly features for making payments.
- It proposes selective identity sharing, integrating contact keys into Bolt 12 offers and BIP21 URIs.
- Feedback is sought to refine selective identity standards for better privacy and usability.
